mission.title: Install and Configure Metal3 Io on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Bare Metal Operator provides a Kubernetes API for managing bare metal hosts, enabling users to provision and manage physical servers as if they were virtual machines. Installing this operator allows you to integrate bare metal provisioning into your Kubernetes environment seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Metal3 Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Metal3 Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add metal3 https://charts.metal3.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Bare Metal Operator
steps.description:
Install the Bare Metal Operator using Helm. This command will create the necessary namespace and deploy the operator:
```bash
helm install baremetal-operator metal3/baremetal-operator --namespace metal3 --create-namespace --version v0.12.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Bare Metal Operator pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3
```

steps.title: Configure RBAC for Bare Metal Operator
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow the Bare Metal Operator to manage resources:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: metal3
  name: baremetal-operator-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["metal3.io"]
  resources: ["baremetalhosts"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete"]
EOF

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: baremetal-operator-rolebinding
  namespace: metal3
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: metal3
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: baremetal-operator-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
```

steps.title: Post-installation health check
steps.description:
Ensure that the Bare Metal Operator is functioning correctly by checking the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=baremetal-operator -n metal3
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Bare Metal Operator pods running in the 'metal3' namespace and the RBAC configuration applied correctly. You can also verify the logs to ensure there are no errors.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add metal3 https://charts.metal3.io
helm repo update
helm install baremetal-operator metal3/baremetal-operator --namespace metal3 --create-namespace --version v0.12.2

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: metal3
  name: baremetal-operator-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["metal3.io"]
  resources: ["baremetalhosts"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete"]
EOF

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: baremetal-operator-rolebinding
  namespace: metal3
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: metal3
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: baremetal-operator-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Bare Metal Operator release using Helm to remove the deployment from the cluster. This command will also remove the associated resources created by the release.
```bash
helm uninstall baremetal-operator --namespace metal3
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) related to the Bare Metal Operator and clean up any persistent volume claims (PVCs) if applicable. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd baremetalhosts.metal3.io
kubectl delete pvc --all -n metal3
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace used for the Bare Metal Operator and verify that it has been deleted. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace metal3
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Bare Metal Operator and any associated resources. You can use the following command to export the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metal3 -o yaml > baremetal-operator-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Bare Metal Operator to the latest version. Use the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade baremetal-operator metal3/baremetal-operator --namespace metal3 --version v0.12.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Bare Metal Operator pods are running the new version successfully. Use the following command to verify:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Bare Metal Operator pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. Use the following command to diagnose:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=baremetal-operator -n metal3
```
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be causing the crash.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission denied errors, ensure that the Role and RoleBinding are correctly configured. Check the Role and RoleBinding with:
```bash
kubectl get role -n metal3
kubectl get rolebinding -n metal3
```
If necessary, recreate them using the provided configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Operator not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the Bare Metal Operator is not responding, check the status of the deployment and pods:
```bash
kubectl get deployment baremetal-operator -n metal3
kubectl get pods -n metal3
```
Ensure that the pods are running and not in a failed state.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive errors related to missing Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs), ensure that the CRDs were installed correctly. Check the CRDs with:
```bash
kubectl get crd
```
If the CRDs are missing, reinstall them using the Helm chart or manually apply the CRD definitions.

prerequisites.description: You need a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm package manager installed.
